description_long: This is a smooth, rounded stone with a dark gray base color and prominent white veining that curves and wraps around its surface. The stone has an almost oval, pebble-like shape, with a slightly flattened profile and gentle contours. The white veins are irregular, some thick and some thin, creating a striking contrast against the dark background and giving the stone a marbled appearance. The surface appears finely polished, suggesting it has been naturally smoothed by water or human intervention. There are no visible cracks, chips, or rough patches, indicating minimal weathering and a well-preserved exterior. Its size and form suggest it could be used as a decorative or functional element in urban landscaping, such as in parks or along pathways.
description: A smooth, dark gray stone with striking white veining and a polished, rounded shape. Its marbled pattern and even surface make it visually distinctive and suitable for decorative urban use.
description_view_0: The stone appears mostly dark gray with several thin, curved white veins running across the visible surface.
description_view_1: This side shows a bold, thick white vein running vertically, dominating the otherwise dark gray surface.
description_view_2: A more subdued view with a single, thin white vein and mostly uninterrupted dark gray surface.
description_view_3: Multiple thin white veins are visible, running vertically and spaced apart on the dark gray background.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular, natural white veining with varying thickness, creating a marbled and organic pattern across the stone's surface.
attribute_weathering: Smooth and polished, with no visible cracks or roughness, indicating minimal weathering and possible water or manual polishing.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts gently with man-made surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements, often placed as a visual accent or boundary marker.
